# Preview: USA vs Australia - prediction, team news, lineups

By Ellis Stevens| 17 Jun 2026 13:32, Last updated: 18 Jun 2026 11:21

© Imago / Icon Sportswire

USA and Australia will bid to maintain their perfect starts in Group D of the 2026 World Cup when they meet on Friday at the Seattle Stadium.

The Stars and Stripes secured a superb 4-1 victory over Paraguay on matchday one, while the Socceroos recorded a surprise 2-0 win against Turkey in their opening game.

---

## Match preview

The USA were the subject of intense discussion ahead of the 2026 World Cup, with many questioning whether Mauricio Pochettino's side could make a deep run in the competition.

There were encouraging signs in the USA's warm-up fixtures, including a 3-2 win against Senegal and a narrow 2-1 loss to Germany, and they carried that momentum into their opening game of the tournament.

The Stars and Stripes were on the front foot from the first whistle, and it took them just seven minutes to take the lead when Damian Bobadilla turned into his own net.

Featured Betting Off

By Ellis Stevens| 17 Jun 2026 13:32, Last updated: 18 Jun 2026 11:21

© Imago / Icon Sportswire

USA and Australia will bid to maintain their perfect starts in Group D of the 2026 World Cup when they meet on Friday at the Seattle Stadium.

The Stars and Stripes secured a superb 4-1 victory over Paraguay on matchday one, while the Socceroos recorded a surprise 2-0 win against Turkey in their opening game.

---

## Match preview

The USA were the subject of intense discussion ahead of the 2026 World Cup, with many questioning whether Mauricio Pochettino's side could make a deep run in the competition.

There were encouraging signs in the USA's warm-up fixtures, including a 3-2 win against Senegal and a narrow 2-1 loss to Germany, and they carried that momentum into their opening game of the tournament.

The Stars and Stripes were on the front foot from the first whistle, and it took them just seven minutes to take the lead when Damian Bobadilla turned into his own net.

# USA vs. Australia prediction, lineups, odds and bet builder tips for World Cup Group D match

Kyle Bonn

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

USA can follow co-host Mexico's lead and secure qualification to the 2026 World Cup knockout stage against Austria in Group D tonight.

They recorded a 4-1 win over Paraguay in their opener but saw Christian Pulisic withdrawn at half time after taking a kick to the calf. It is unclear whether the star man has trained with the team, and Mauricio Pochettino could opt for caution due to Australia's perceived bully-ball style.

# USMNT projected World Cup lineup vs. Australia: If Christian Pulisic can't go, who replaces USA Soccer's star?

## With a spot in the knockout stage on the line, the USMNT may have to face Australia without captain Christian Pulisic due to a calf issue

By Chuck Booth

21 hrs ago • 3 min read

Add CBS Sports on Google

Getty Images

While the World Cup vibes around the United States men's national team couldn't be higher after their victory to kick off the group stage against Paraguay, there is unfortunately one cause for concern as they gear up to face Australia. The USMNT can guarantee their place in the knockout stages with a victory in that match, but they may need to do so without Christian Pulisic.

Pulisic was subbed out following the first half of the victory over Paraguay due to a calf issue that he has been deal

news_items.title: USMNT vs. Australia—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups
news_items.text:
USMNT vs. Australia—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups

After cruising to victory in their respective 2026 World Cup openers, the U.S. men’s national team and Australia will battle for the top spot in Group D on Friday.

The Stars and Stripes came out with a point to prove against Paraguay last week, romping to a 4–1 victory that had fans at SoFi Stadium and across the country bursting with pride. There were few flaws to pick out from such a dominant performance, and the USMNT will be raring to make another statement seven days later, this time at Lumen Field in Seattle.

Standing in their way of another three points is an Australia side fresh off a shock 2–0 victory over Türkiye. Tony Popovic’s men were on the scoresheet before the half-hour mark and never let up, holding Arda Güler and Co. quiet to claim second place in the group, only behind the USMNT on goal differential.

The Socceroos will be eager to avenge their 2–1 defeat to Mauricio Pochettino’s men from the October international break, but they have their work cut out against a U.S. team that looks reborn on the world stage.

Compete against the world. | SPORTS ILLUSTRATED

---

## USMNT vs. Australia Score Pred

# Preview: USA vs Australia - prediction, team news, lineups

By Ellis Stevens| 17 Jun 2026 13:32, Last updated: 18 Jun 2026 11:21

© Imago / Icon Sportswire

USA and Australia will bid to maintain their perfect starts in Group D of the 2026 World Cup when they meet on Friday at the Seattle Stadium.

The Stars and Stripes secured a superb 4-1 victory over Paraguay on matchday one, while the Socceroos recorded a surprise 2-0 win against Turkey in their opening game.

---

## Match preview

The USA were the subject of intense discussion ahead of the 2026 World Cup, with many questioning whether Mauricio Pochettino's side could make a deep run in the competition.

There were encouraging signs in the USA's warm-up fixtures, including a 3-2 win against Senegal and a narrow 2-1 loss to Germany, and they carried that momentum into their opening game of the tournament.

The Stars and Stripes were on the front foot from the first whistle, and it took them just seven minutes to take the lead when Damian Bobadilla turned into his own net.

Featured Betting Off"}

USA can follow co-host Mexico's lead and secure qualification to the 2026 World Cup knockout stage against Austria in Group D tonight.

# USMNT projected World Cup lineup vs. Australia: If Christian Pulisic can't go, who replaces USA Soccer's star?

## With a spot in the knockout stage on the line, the USMNT may have to face Australia without captain Christian Pulisic due to a calf issue

By Chuck Booth

21 hrs ago • 3 min read

Add CBS Sports on Google

Getty Images

While the World Cup vibes around the United States men's national team couldn't be higher after their victory to kick off the group stage against Paraguay, there is unfortunately one cause for concern as they gear up to face Australia. The USMNT can guarantee their place in the knockout stages with a victory in that match, but they may need to do so without Christian Pulisic.

Pulisic was subbed out following the first half of the victory over Paraguay due to a calf issue that he has been deal"}
{"title": "USMNT vs. Australia—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups", "url": "https://www.si.com/soccer/usmnt-vs-australia-world-cup-preview-predictions-lineups-6-19-26", "publishedDate": "2026-06-18T23:41:47.000Z", "author": null, "text": "USMNT vs. Australia—World Cup: Preview, Predictions and Lineups

After cruising to victory in their respective 2026 World Cup openers, the U.S. men’s national team and Australia will battle for the top spot in Group D on Friday.

The Stars and Stripes came out with a point to prove against Paraguay last week, romping to a 4–1 victory that had fans at SoFi Stadium and across the country bursting with pride. There were few flaws to pick out from such a dominant performance, and the USMNT will be raring to make another statement seven days later, this time at Lumen Field in Seattle.

Standing in their way of another three points is an Australia side fresh off a shock 2–0 victory over Türkiye. Tony Popovic’s men were on the scoresheet before the half-hour mark and never let up, holding Arda Güler and Co. quiet to claim second place in the group, only behind the USMNT on goal differential.

The Socceroos will be eager to avenge their 2–1 defeat to Mauricio Pochettino’s men from the October international break, but they have their work cut out against a U.S. team that looks reborn on the world stage.
